news.headline: 94% of Nova Scotia school support staff vote to strike, 5,000 workers demanding better treatment in public education.
news.summary: In Nova Scotia, school support staff in eight CUPE locals have voted in favor of job action, with a significant 94% of workers supporting a strike mandate. This decision, made by 5,000 workers, reflects their unwavering commitment to their demands and solidarity. The outcome of the strike votes, concluded last week, highlights the prevailing issues in public education in the province, as mentioned by Nan McFadgen, President of the union.

news.headline: Oct 23, 2024: Oriental Rise Holding Limited’s underwriter, US Tiger Securities, exercises full over-allotment option buying 262,500 shares at $4 each, raising $8.05M total.
news.summary: Ningde, China, October 23, 2024 - Oriental Rise Holding Limited, a leading tea product supplier in China listed as ORIS on NasdaqCM, disclosed that US Tiger Securities, Inc. served as the underwriter and sole book-runner for their IPO. They recently exercised the full over-allotment option by purchasing an additional 262,500 ordinary shares from the Company at $4.00 per share, increasing the gross proceeds to $8.05 million before deductions for underwriting discounts and expenses. This move followed the issuance of a total of 2,012,500 ordinary shares during the IPO.

news.headline: Aspira Women’s Health awarded $10 million by ARPA-H to develop blood test for endometriosis detection.
news.summary: Aspira is working on creating a blood test using multiple miRNA markers for the detection of endometriosis. This innovative test aims to improve early detection of the condition, which affects approximately 1 in 10 women of reproductive age. The use of miRNA markers may enhance the accuracy of diagnosis and lead to timely treatment for individuals with endometriosis. By developing this test, Aspira seeks to provide a valuable tool for healthcare professionals in identifying and managing this common gynecological disorder.

news.headline: Exact Market, a woman-owned tech marketing agency, enhances B2B video services to meet growing demand for digital storytelling.

news.headline: Winnebago Industries (WGO) Q4: EPS 28c vs. street view 89c, sales $720.9M beats $719.14M consensus. Stock down on soft outlook, gross profit margin down 340bps. CEO blames retail demand and inefficiencies.
news.summary: Winnebago Industries, Inc. (NYSE:WGO) experienced a downturn in its shares following the release of its fourth-quarter results. The company disclosed adjusted earnings per share at 28 cents, falling short of the anticipated 89 cents. Although quarterly sales amounted to $720.9 million, surpassing the predicted $719.14 million by analysts, the gross profit saw a 26.1% decrease year over year, reaching $94.2 million. The gross profit margin also took a hit, dropping by 340 basis points to 13.1% due to increased warranty expenses, operational issues, and deleverage. President and CEO Michael Happe cited the underwhelming performance in the fourth quarter to sluggish retail demand and operational inefficiencies. More details on the story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Florida's housing market sees increased inventory and stable prices in Sept 2024, compared to year ago.

news.headline: Caesars Entertainment completes $435M transformation of Caesars New Orleans, celebrated at event with top executives.
